Majestic Care of Hopemont

CCN: 51E148 · TERRA ALTA, WV 26764 · Preston County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
150 HOPEMONT DRIVE, TERRA ALTA, WV 26764
Phone
3047892411
Certified beds
98
Avg daily residents
49 (50% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (state)
Provider type
Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1988-03-30
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.59 3.01 5.87 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.95 0.53 1.22 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.68 0.67 0.87 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.96 1.82 3.79 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.63 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.19
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.70
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
5.36
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
40.7%
annual
RN turnover
36.4%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.07
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.78
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-06-06 16 16 1 120 1 120
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-08-24 18 6 12 612 1 612
Total weighted health score
243.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (43)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

38 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
5 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-11-20 2025-01-01
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-11-20 2025-01-01
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-11-20 2025-01-01
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-06-06 2024-07-30
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-03-27 2024-04-15
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-03-27 2024-04-15
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-03-27 2024-04-15
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. D 2024-03-27 2024-04-15
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. L 2024-02-09 2024-03-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. L 2024-02-09 2024-03-22
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. L 2024-02-09 2024-03-22
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. L 2024-02-09 2024-01-07
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. L 2024-02-09 2024-01-07
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2022-08-24 2022-10-19
0680 Ensure the activities program is directed by a qualified professional. E 2022-08-24 2022-10-19
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2022-08-24 2022-10-19
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2022-08-24 2022-10-19
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2022-08-24 2022-10-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2022-08-24 2022-10-19
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. D 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2021-04-30 2021-06-03
